title: pimelea lehmanniana
intro: Pimelea lehmanniana, also known as Lemon-scented Rice Flower, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Pimelea lehmanniana is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
